items.title: Vision loss and self-harm — government warned of custody health risks

children.content: Internal government memos obtained by the ABC detail a series of health incidents in Northern Territory watch houses and a suicide attempt in the Holtze prison.

gallery.altText: Glass jail walls and a red duress button

thumbnail.altText: Glass jail walls and a red duress button

items.title: Man jailed for stabbing wife to death eligible for parole in two weeks

children.content: Dennis Naroldol, 59, has been sentenced to four years in prison over the fatal stabbing of his wife outside the Darwin DoubleTree Hilton in 2023 and will be eligible for parole in two weeks after time served.

image.altText: Man in a blue shirt is interviewed for TV

items.title: NT fuel retailers forced to reveal full costs as 77yo law revived

children.content: The Northern Territory government has triggered a decades old law in an attempt to combat fuel price gouging at the bowser.

image.altText: A person paying for fuel at petrol station

items.title: Hundreds of thousands in flood damages leave NT tourism vendors devastated

children.content: After multiple major weather events, tourism vendors are looking at clean-up costs in the hundreds of thousands, with some businesses lost entirely. The industry is calling for governments to step in and help.

gallery.altText: A reception area full of mud, with flood debris over the counter.

thumbnail.altText: A reception area full of mud, with flood debris over the counter.

avatar.alt: A journalist holding a notebook and microphone, speaking to the camera.

items.title: Artists left devastated after floods destroy renowned NT arts centre

children.content: Artists from the remote Northern Territory community of Nauiyu are facing a devastating loss after the Merrepen Arts Centre was destroyed by recent flooding.

image.altText: Kieren Karritpul, an Indigenous man wearing a pattern shirt, stands in front of several paintings hanging on the wall.

tags.title: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander Art

items.title: 19yo charged with murder after fatal stabbing in Katherine 'fist fight'

children.content: Police have charged a 19-year-old man with murder after he allegedly stabbed two other teenagers in Katherine on Sunday, leaving one dead and the other with critical injuries.

image.altText: Two police vehicles on street road, crime scene tape in front of cars, dark night, flashing lights on one car.

items.title: The ABC rode along with paramedics to examine the NT service's under-resourcing

children.content: Warren Purse, a Territory paramedic with over 20 years' experience, is assaulted on the job about three or four times a year.

image.altText: Mid-shot, white man, paramedic uniform, leaning against ambulance, arms crossed, gray hair serious expression.

articles.title: St John NT warns 'extraordinary' weekend measures 'absolutely not' safe

children.content: Northern Territory paramedics pushed "well and truly beyond our limits" at the weekend say the "extraordinary" measures taken in response are unsustainable and "absolutely not" safe.

image.altText: Female paramedic looks ahead with serious face

articles.title: Darwin's ambulance service fails overnight as 61 Triple Zero calls abandoned

children.content: Darwin's ambulance service has reached a "critical failure point" as 42 per cent of Triple Zero calls were abandoned overnight.

image.altText: Triple Zero emergency sign on St John NT ambulance

gallery.altText: Mid-shot, white man, paramedic uniform, leaning against ambulance, arms crossed, gray hair serious expression.

thumbnail.altText: Mid-shot, white man, paramedic uniform, leaning against ambulance, arms crossed, gray hair serious expression.

items.title: Darwin records second Indigenous death in custody within a week

children.content: In less than a week, another Indigenous man in his 20s has died in custody in Darwin. The incidents were not related, but the cause of death for both men remains unknown.

image.altText: An aerial photo of a correctional facility.

items.title: Convenience stores selling illegal tobacco in Darwin spark organised crime fears

children.content: Convenience stores selling cheap, illegally packaged cigarettes have been popping up across Darwin and businesses are worried that organised crime and gang violence are next.

image.altText: Convenience store with red and black signage

images.altText: Donald Trump speaks at the Oval Office and holds up his right hand in an almost pinching motion to show size

articles.title: American journalist Shelly Kittleson kidnapped in Baghdad

children.content: Police in Iraq say they are searching for an American journalist who has been kidnapped in Baghdad, possibly by an Iranian-backed militia group.

image.altText: Shelly Kittleson stands in front of a sunset and the site of ruins.

articles.title: Trump tells allies 'go get your own oil' from Strait of Hormuz

children.content: The US president again attacks America's allies over their reluctance to get involved in the Iran war, warning them the "US won't be there to help you anymore, just like you weren't there for us".

image.altText: Donald Trump wearing a blue suit and tie gesturing with his right hand while standing at a US presidential lectern

children.content: US President Donald Trump says the US will leave Iran "very soon" has given an estimate of "two weeks, maybe three". Follow live.

items.title: Lawsuit filed against Taylor Swift over The Life of a Showgirl album

children.content: Las Vegas performer Maren Wade says the name and imagery of Taylor Swift's latest album are too close to those of her show, Confessions of a Showgirl.

image.altText: Taylor Swift poses in a body of water in a glittering silver corset

items.title: Mine may be shut down amid money laundering allegations, bikie claims

children.content: The Tasmanian government has taken steps to revoke the operating licence of one of the state's biggest mining operations, after being warned it was allegedly used as a "vehicle for money laundering" and former owners were associated with underworld figures.

image.altText: A graphic of a mining site and a dark figure of a man in a bike helmet.

items.title: Man pleads guilty to murder over body found in buried freezer

children.content: Bobby Weaver pleaded guilty to murder more than seven years after the death of 58-year-old David Thornton, whose body was found buried in a freezer in his Ipswich backyard.

image.altText: A delapidated freezer in a pit

items.title: 'It's tragic': Hundreds to be impacted after seafood giant folds

children.content: Australia's largest wild-caught prawn operation is to close its doors after 60 years in business, impacting hundreds of workers and devastating a Queensland community.

image.altText: raptis fleets docked at the karumba wharf

items.title: ASX jumps on back of massive Wall St rally amid war end hopes

children.content: Wall Street ends a weak month with a massive gain, as investors eye a possible end to US military action in Iran, and Australian shares follow. Follow the day's events and insights from our business reporters on the ABC News live markets blog.

image.altText: A close up of Donald Trump.

items.title: Spurs fans protest over new manager as post-Postecoglou woes deepen

children.content: Tottenham fan groups voice opposition to newly appointed manager Roberto De Zerbi over his previous public support for Mason Greenwood, a player who faced charges of attempted rape and assault.

image.altText: Roberto De Zerbi sits on the bench and looks to his left

items.title: 'We are an expert': How Lebanon's airport keeps running despite Israeli strikes

children.content: Beirut-Rafic Hariri Airport, Lebanon's gateway to the world, is still operating even as Israel bombards its surroundings.

gallery.altText: An airport hall with lined floor, seats, a glass ceiling, and no people around

items.title: How this Aussie went from TAFE to the head of Sony character animation

children.content: Daniel Pozo has had a hand in Sony hits like Spiderman: Across the Spider-Verse and Hotel Transylvania, but GOAT is his first project in the driver's seat.

children.content: A new orchestral Bluey album is out. Lead composer Joff Bush talks about classical music, storytelling and inspiring kids to love music.

image.altText: A man in a colourful shirt plays the melodica

items.title: Western Australia at risk of mouse plague 'spiralling out of control'

children.content: Grain paddocks in Western Australia are filled with thousands of mice which are making their way into residential areas.

image.altText: A metal mouse trap filled with mice.

items.title: Socceroos headliners shine, but will the experimentation pay off at World Cup?

children.content: The Socceroos are done with experimenting, but now need to find the winning formula with the World Cup quickly approaching.

articles.title: Italy fails to reach third straight World Cup after penalty shootout loss

children.content: Italy's World Cup exile continues after the four-time champions fall to a 4-1 penalty shootout defeat by Bosnia and Herzegovina.

image.altText: Pio Esposito reacts after missing a penalty against Bosnia and Herzegovina.

articles.title: Socceroos thump Curaçao 5-1 in World Cup farewell, as Irankunda electrifies

items.title: Dire future of superb fairy-wren could mean catastrophe for other species

children.content: The researchers said the superb fairy-wren would likely go extinct within 30-40 years unless action was taken to stop greenhouse gas emissions causing climate change.

image.altText: A small blue-headed wren sitting in a tree.

items.title: Could I be using E10 in my car?

children.content: Australia's leading motoring organisation is urging drivers to fill up with E10 fuel in an effort to conserve national fuel supplies. But what exactly is E10? And could it help ease the current fuel crisis?

image.altText: A man holds a petrol bowser while filling up his car.

items.title: Australia expected to create more solar panel waste by 2030

children.content: Australians are expected to throw away 90,000 tonnes of solar panel waste each year by 2030, a federal government hearing has been told.

image.altText: Four rows of dark solar panels on a silver roof.

items.title: House prices rise everywhere besides Sydney and Melbourne

children.content: There is a significant divide between property price growth in major cities as buyers weigh up interest rate hikes and geopolitical tensions.

image.altText: A man and a woman smile at the camera.

items.title: Farmers think outside the box as rising fuel costs add to drought woes

children.content: Farmers are turning to roadside grazing, selling up stock, and, in one case, shipping cattle to an island to navigate the drought and the rising cost of diesel.

image.altText: A young woman in a brown hat smiles to camera, with cattle grazing the roadside in the background.

items.title: Shein and Kmart accused of copying Queensland retailer's designs

children.content: Queensland business Sabo Skirt has claimed Shein, Kmart and numerous other retailers and wholesalers have copied its clothing patterns, prints and trademarks on "lower quality" garments in a federal court claim.

image.altText: A graphic with a dress with an aquatic print and a Sabo logo next to Shein and Kmart logos over aquatic print garments.

items.title: Cocoa prices crash but Easter chocolate stays expensive

children.content: Consumers cannot expect to find cheaper chocolate on supermarket shelves this Easter despite a major drop in the world cocoa price, industry experts say.

gallery.altText: Close up of small easter eggs in a bowl.

thumbnail.altText: Close up of small easter eggs in a bowl.

avatar.alt: A woman with blonde hair smiles at the camera. She's wearing black.

items.title: One of Australia's biggest 'giveaway' clubs has its licence cancelled

children.content: RS Rewards claims to have given away more than $10 million worth of cars, houses and cash. But its licence has just been cancelled.

image.altText: A luxurious house on the gold coast with a large pool, surrounded by palm trees.

items.title: Police allege murder victim's body was set alight after death

children.content: A 37-year-old man has been arrested and charged with murder a week after burning human remains were found at a Dimbulah property.

image.altText: A police officer standing next to a trailer in a backyard.

items.title: Childhood vaccine coverage declines for fifth year in a row

children.content: The latest immunisation data shows more than 80,000 Australian children aged five and under are not fully up to date with their vaccinations.

image.altText: A nurse holds a needle up to a young boy's arm

items.title: This controversial rom-com starring Zendaya is one of the best movies of the year

children.content: On the surface, A-listers Zendaya and Robert Pattinson's new film seems like a regular rom-com. That's exactly what director Kristoffer Borgli wants you to think.

image.altText: A man dips a woman while they smile at each other

items.title: 'Nothing's out here' Vodafone ad angers rural Australians

children.content: A new Vodafone campaign has regional Australians seething after the telecommunications giant claimed to focus its network infrastructure on where "98 whatever per cent of you actually live".
